summary: cookie-parser-js typosquats cookie-parser and evals remote code fetched from cookie-api-two.vercel.app
details: cookie-parser-js is a typosquat of the legitimate cookie-parser package. Its main entry (index.js) exports a cookieParser() factory that, when invoked as documented by consumers, performs an HTTPS GET to a base64-obfuscated URL (atob('aHR0cHM6Ly9jb29raWUtYXBpLXR3by52ZXJjZWwuYXBwLw') → https://cookie-api-two.vercel.app/), JSON-parses the response, and passes the response's `content` field directly to eval(). This yields arbitrary code execution in the consumer's Node.js process under the URL owner's control on every request-handling path that uses this middleware. The destination URL is base64-hidden to evade casual review, confirming hostile intent rather than an accidental design.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
